purpose: The cross-client player identity palette (DESIGN.md §8). The server ships each player a stable wire color (#RRGGBB, apps/session color.ts colorForUser: an FNV-1a hash of the userId). A client does not paint that raw hash; it buckets the wire color to a slot via (value % 12) and paints the slot's light- or dark-ground variant. Frozen here so web (apps/web identityRoster.ts), push (apps/session push/roster.ts), and iOS (apps/ios IdentityRoster.swift) render the same player the same color. The dark column is the one apps/session already ships; the light column is iOS's, adopted here as the shared source.

slotForWireColor.intent: the low three bytes taken mod 12; #000000 is slot 0
a roster hex does not round-trip to its own slot; #9D95FF (slot 0's dark) buckets to 3
